title: Xtra Crisp Russian Chicken Skewers
description: A quick-fix Russian dish featuring coconut cream and clay-pot cooking, this recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner and is Buddhist-temple-friendly, charred and crisp on the outside and juicy on the inside, these skewers are a delight to eat.
instructions: Preheat the clay pot over medium-high heat.
Thread the chicken pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
In a small bowl, whisk together the coconut cream, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the mixture onto the chicken skewers, making sure they are fully coated.
Place the skewers in the clay pot and cook for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and slightly charred.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Let the skewers rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve with your favorite sides, such as roasted vegetables or quinoa.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
